risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Differences in detection-method remediation rates are a contributing_factor to overall detection/response risk (R-Information security operations) because they influence time-to-remediate and residual exposure but do not alone quantify exploitability.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: If a normally high-remediation detection method suddenly yields low remediation rates or high-severity findings spike for one method (trends include 'detection volume shift by method'), that's an anomaly_pattern indicating a tooling or process regression.

metrics.name: Vulnerability recurrence lifespan
metrics.description: Measures the lifespan and recurrence of vulnerabilities by name: time from first_detected_at to last_detected_at and frequency of reappearance. Identifies unstable fixes or recurring exposures.
kpis: average recurrence count proxy (presence across time windows)
trends: count of recurring vulnerabilities over time
metrics.natural_language_question: Which vulnerability names recur most frequently or remain present longest across our environment?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: VPM-2 and OPS-1 expect scan history and remediation tracking (data_signals include detection timestamps and remediation tickets); KPIs such as 'percent recurring > 90 days' derived from first_detected_at/last_detected_at directly evidence remediation completeness and stability.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Recurrence lifespan is a direct measurement of ongoing exposure and remediation failure modes relevant to operations risk (R-Information security operations) because it uses recurrence counts and durations as signals.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A rising percent recurring >90d or an uptick in median recurrence lifespan (KPIs and trends) constitutes a distribution_shift that indicates regressions in remediation or recurring exploit vectors.

metrics.name: Remediation & related-issue documentation coverage
metrics.description: Measures the percent of findings that include remediation instructions, related_issues links, or portal_url references. Good documentation coverage accelerates triage and remediation.
kpis: average time to link remediation (where trackable)
metrics.natural_language_question: How many findings include actionable remediation or tracking links, and where are documentation gaps by severity?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: VPM-2 expects remediation tracking and OPS/scan evidence; remediation_documentation_coverage provides remediation links and portal URLs (data_filters include remediation, related_issues, portal_url) which map to VPM-2 and to external assessment evidence (IAO-2) when used alongside remediation ticket linkage.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Documentation coverage is a contributing_factor to remediation effectiveness and thus to operational security risk — missing docs slow remediation but don't directly measure exploitability.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A drop below a policy threshold in 'percent with remediation guidance' or spike in 'percent findings without remediation links' would indicate a threshold_breach signalling documentation gaps that impede remediation.

metrics.name: CVE description & metadata coverage
metrics.description: Assesses completeness of CVE-related metadata (cve_description, nvd_severity, cvssv2/v3/v4) across findings to identify enrichment gaps for triage and scoring systems.
kpis: percent with at least one CVSS vector
metrics.natural_language_question: Which findings lack CVE descriptions or CVSS/NVD metadata that we can enrich to improve prioritization?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: OPS-1 and VPM-2 expect vulnerability scan and remediation evidence; CVE/metadata completeness (data_filters include cve_description, cvssv3/v4, nvd_severity) supports scoring and prioritization requirements in those controls though stronger evidence includes linkage to scan/enrichment jobs and proof of enrichment automation.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Metadata coverage is a proxy_indicator for the organization's ability to accurately prioritize vulnerabilities (R-Information security operations) because missing metadata hampers direct exposure calculation.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: If enrichment pipelines fail, the metric will show an absence of CVE descriptions or CVSS vectors (KPIs drop), representing an absence_detection that enrichment jobs are not producing expected metadata.

metrics.name: Average findings per evidence artifact
metrics.description: Calculates the mean and distribution of finding_ids associated with each EvidenceArtifact, indicating how many findings each artifact documents. This matters for understanding evidence richness and whether artifacts represent single or aggregated findings.
metrics.natural_language_question: Are evidence artifacts increasingly consolidating multiple findings into single artifacts, and does this vary by artifact_type?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: This metric uses finding_ids and artifact mappings (source_schemas: wiz_knowledge_repo/reporting_posture) which align to OPS-1 (vulnerability monitoring) and VPM-2 (host-based scanning/remediation tracking) evidence needs, but average findings-per-artifact is an evidence-quality indicator rather than raw vulnerability scan output, so strength is moderate.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Average findings-per-artifact is a proxy for visibility and aggregation practices that affect CVE exposure and unpatched-systems visibility; it does not directly count open CVEs per asset, so it is a proxy indicator for those risks.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A measurable change in the distribution (e.g., median_findings_per_artifact increasing or top artifact_type concentration shifting) would indicate consolidation changes or tooling changes; the metric's trends include 'distribution shift', supporting this detection mechanism.

metrics.name: Unique findings with associated artifacts
metrics.description: Counts unique finding_ids captured across EvidenceArtifacts per organization and period, indicating how many distinct findings have supporting artifacts. Tracking this helps measure coverage of findings by evidence.
metrics.natural_language_question: How many unique findings have at least one evidence artifact in the last 30 days for each organization?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: Counting unique findings with artifacts (source_schemas and KPIs) directly supports VPM-2 (host-based scanning and remediation tracking) and OPS-1 (vulnerability management and monitoring), which expect vulnerability-to-evidence linkage and remediation ticket timestamps; therefore this is strong evidence for those controls.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: The count of unique findings that have supporting artifacts is a direct measurement of coverage for vulnerability findings (it directly quantifies how many findings are evidence-backed) and therefore maps to ops/asset risks.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: If unique_findings_with_artifacts_count drops below an expected coverage threshold (KPI and natural_language_question focus on last 30 days), that breach of coverage threshold would be detected as a threshold_breach indicating visibility/ingestion problems.

metrics.name: Attack path density by asset
metrics.description: Measures how many distinct attack paths originate from or reach an asset (density), showing which assets are involved in many paths and therefore increase exposure blast radius. Helps prioritize defensive controls for high-density assets.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which assets act as frequent sources of attack paths and how many unique targets can each source reach?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: This metric directly measures attack-path density and unique targets (data_signals from exposures_attack_paths) which strongly aligns to OPS-1 (vulnerability management monitoring) and NET-2 (segmentation/topology controls) because it quantifies how many paths traverse assets and segments — direct evidence for those controls.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Attack-path counts and unique target reach are direct indicators of operational security exposure and asset-related risk (large blast-radius hubs). KPIs like 'unique_targets_per_source' map to risk indicators.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A sudden spike in attack_paths_per_source or emergence of many unique targets for an asset (NLQ asks 'Which assets act as frequent sources') would indicate an anomalous expansion in attack reach (spike_detection).

metrics.name: Attack path persistence and recurrence
metrics.description: Measures how long attack paths persist and how often the same path is observed (recurrence), indicating unstable or unresolved attack vectors. Persistent or recurring paths are high priority for remediation or network segmentation.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which attack paths persist the longest or recur most frequently within each organization?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: Attack-path persistence/recurrence directly maps to OPS-1 scanning/monitoring and NET-2 segmentation evidence: persistence metrics (avg_path_persistence_days, recurrence_rate) are strong signals that remediation or segmentation controls are failing or incomplete.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Path persistence is a direct measure of ongoing operational exposure and increased likelihood of impact; KPIs measure persistence directly tied to operational risk indicators.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: An increase in path_recurrence_rate or lengthening avg_path_persistence_days (NLQ: 'Which attack paths persist the longest') would appear as a shift in the persistence distribution, indicating unresolved attack vectors.

metrics.name: Asset centrality in attack paths
metrics.description: Counts how often an asset appears as a source or target across attack paths to identify hubs that concentrate risk and could amplify compromise. Central assets are good candidates for additional hardening or monitoring.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which assets act as central hubs in attack paths and what percentage of total paths do the top 10 assets account for?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: Asset centrality (how often an asset appears in paths) provides strong evidence for vulnerability management (OPS-1) and for segmentation controls (NET-2) because high centrality indicates where segmentation or additional controls are required; the metric's KPIs directly quantify these signals.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Centrality percentage and avg degree are direct measures of how assets concentrate attack paths and therefore directly quantify exposure and asset-related operational risk.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: The 'emergence of new hubs' and 'centrality shift over time' trends described would manifest as a distribution shift in centrality scores (e.g., previously low-degree assets moving into top_n_central_assets).

metrics.name: Attack path overlap with exposed assets
metrics.description: Fraction and count of attack paths that include at least one asset currently recorded as exposed, showing how exposures contribute to exploitable pathing. Identifies how exposures map into the actual attack graph.
metrics.natural_language_question: What fraction of observed attack paths include at least one asset currently recorded as exposed and how has that fraction changed recently?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: Fraction/count of attack paths including exposed assets directly evidences OPS-1's requirement to link vulnerability/exposure findings to operational risk and NET-2's segmentation/zone isolation effectiveness; the metric's kpis are direct attack-graph to exposure mappings (strong evidence).

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: The metric directly measures the portion of the attack graph that is influenced by exposures (pct_attack_paths_including_exposed_asset), directly quantifying operational and communications-related exposure.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: An increase in the fraction of paths touching exposed assets (NLQ: 'how has that fraction changed recently?') would appear as a distribution shift, signaling exposures are increasingly exploitable.

metrics.name: Exposure-to-attack-path lag distribution
metrics.description: Distribution of the time lag between an exposure first_seen and the first observed attack path involving the same asset (source or target), highlighting how quickly exposures are leveraged in attack paths. Short lags indicate rapid exploitation windows that demand fast remediation.
metrics.natural_language_question: For exposures that lead to attack paths, what is the typical time between exposure detection and the first observed attack path?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: Median lag and pct_paths_within_7d map directly to OPS-1's vulnerability monitoring/remediation and IRO-1's need to shorten exploitation windows via response testing; the metric's data_signals (first_seen,last_seen, path timestamps) provide strong evidence of exploit timelines.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: The time between exposure detection and first observed attack path is a direct measurement of the exploitation window and thus directly quantifies the immediacy and severity of operational risk.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: An uptick in the pct_paths_within_7d KPI or a drop in median_lag_days below a policy-defined acceptable window would constitute a threshold breach indicating exposures are being exploited more quickly.

metrics.name: Asset Inventory Count
metrics.description: Total count of assets (devices) by type, platform, site, and location. Coverage and inventory health.
metrics.natural_language_question: How many assets do we have by platform and region, and how has our cloud asset count grown in the last quarter?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: AST-3 ('production_asset_inventory_and_lifecycle_management') explicitly requires inventory coverage, ownership/location fields and reconciliation logs; the metric uses asset_schema and agent/host sources that align directly to AST-3 data_signals.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Total and breakdown counts of assets are direct measurements of the asset management risk domain (the metric's kpis and groups explicitly enumerate asset counts and platform/region splits).

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: If asset heartbeats or inventory rows stop appearing (missing devices or sudden drops in counts by site_name/location_region), that absence indicates discovery/collection failure or mass decommissioning/compromise; the metric's agent/host sources and 'last seen' semantics enable absence detection.

metrics.name: Asset Risk Score Distribution
metrics.description: Distribution of asset risk scores (raw_risk, effective_risk) for prioritization and blast radius.
metrics.natural_language_question: What is the distribution of asset risk scores across our environment, and which sites have the highest concentration of high-risk assets?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: AST-3 requires asset inventory and OPS-1 requires vulnerability and monitoring coverage; the metric's source schemas (asset_schema, features.json) and kpis (mean risk score, critical assets) provide relevant signals but asset risk scoring is an aggregated/derived input so evidence is moderate until raw scoring inputs and asset-to-score mapping are validated.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Asset risk scores are synthesized indicators (raw_risk, effective_risk) that serve as proxies for likely impact/blast-radius rather than direct incident measurements — the metric aggregates those scores to quantify exposure.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A sudden redistribution (more assets moving into high-risk buckets or a site developing high-risk concentration) would be detected as a distribution shift; the metric explicitly captures risk_bucket and site_name for this purpose.

metrics.name: Asset Agent Coverage
metrics.description: Assets with agent presence (Balbix, Sentinel, etc.). Coverage gaps and agent health.
metrics.natural_language_question: What percentage of our assets have an active agent, and which sites have the most coverage gaps?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: END-1 requires 'percentage of managed endpoints with an active, up-to-date anti-malware agent' and OPS-1 requires monitoring agent coverage; the metric's source_schemas (asset_agents_schema, asset_schema), kpis and 'last_seen/is_stale' filters map cleanly to those control evidence_requirements.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Agent coverage % is a direct operational measurement of monitoring capability (the metric's KPI and NLQ explicitly measure active-agent percentage rather than a proxy).

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A sudden absence of agent heartbeats (stale count rising or 'Assets without agent' increasing) would indicate monitoring/collection outages or mass deprovisioning; the metric contains 'first_seen/last_seen/is_stale' filters enabling this detection.

metrics.name: Asset Open Ports Summary
metrics.description: Open ports per asset with service and traffic visibility. Attack surface and exposure.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which assets have the most open ports, and are there any newly observed ports in the last 7 days?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: NET-4 ('firewall_configuration_and_coverage') and NET-2 ('network_segmentation_and_zone_isolation') expect evidence on permitted/blocked network flows and segmentation; open-port inventories and traffic-seen fields provide relevant exposure signals, though firewall rule/ACL linkage is needed to reach full control evidence (hence moderate).

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Open port counts and newly observed ports are direct indicators of network exposure and align to communications-security and operations risk indicators (data_groups and KPIs make this explicit).

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: Newly observed ports or a sudden increase in open-port counts per asset (NLQ asks about 'newly observed ports in the last 7 days') would appear as a spike in the port-count time series, enabling rapid detection.

metrics.name: Asset Interface Inventory
metrics.description: Network interfaces per asset (IP, subnet, MAC, site). Network topology and segmentation.
metrics.natural_language_question: How many network interfaces do we have per subnet, and which sites have the most IPs?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: AST-3 requires an authoritative asset inventory and NET-2 requires mapping of customer-data assets to network zones; asset_interface_schema and data_groups (subnet/site) provide the required inventory signals for these controls.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Counts of interfaces and subnet distribution are direct measurements of asset and network topology which feed asset and communications security risk assessments (the NLQ/kpis are inventory-oriented).

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A sudden surge in interfaces per subnet or many new IPs (NLQ asks 'which sites have the most IPs') would appear as a spike in per-subnet interface counts, indicating unexpected provisioning or potential compromise.

metrics.name: Asset Geographic Distribution
metrics.description: Asset count by country, region, city. Geo-fencing and compliance.
metrics.natural_language_question: How are our assets distributed geographically, and do we have any assets in restricted regions?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: AST-3 requires a production asset inventory with location metadata; the metric's source_schemas (asset_schema), source_capabilities (qualys.hosts, wiz.assets_inventory, sentinel.locations) and KPIs (Asset count by country, Restricted region count) supply the required inventory/location data_signals, providing strong evidence for AST-3.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Counting assets by country and identifying assets in restricted regions are direct measurements of asset-management and compliance exposure (KPIs directly capture these counts).

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: No scenarios provided. The metric will detect policy breaches if 'Restricted region count' becomes >0 or crosses allowed thresholds (NLQ specifically asks about restricted regions) — a threshold_breach detection.

metrics.name: Scan Coverage and Freshness
metrics.description: Last scan date per asset, scan coverage gaps. VM/compliance scan health.
metrics.natural_language_question: What percentage of our assets were scanned in the last 7 days, and which sites have the oldest scans?

metrics.name: Asset First/Last Seen
metrics.description: Asset lifecycle timestamps for drift detection and decommission tracking.
metrics.natural_language_question: How many new assets were discovered this week, and which assets haven't been seen in 90 days?

metrics.name: Risk Impact Breakdown
metrics.description: Impact factors (bastion, propagation, category) per asset. Blast radius analysis.
metrics.natural_language_question: What is the distribution of impact scores across our assets, and which have the highest blast radius?

metrics.name: Asset Software Licensing Compliance
metrics.description: Measures installed software against licensed entitlements by site and department to quantify compliance and potential cost exposure. Identifies unlicensed installations, vendor-level noncompliance, and high-risk locations for procurement or audit action.
metrics.natural_language_question: What percentage of installed software is covered by valid licenses by vendor and site, and where are the largest concentrations of unlicensed software?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: AST-3 (production_asset_inventory_and_lifecycle_management) requires a centralized inventory and compares discovered assets to inventory — this metric uses cvedata/asset_schema and software_instances_schema and the KPI licensed_coverage_percent matches AST-3's evidence_requirements (asset inventory, discovered software vs recorded inventory), so it provides strong evidence.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: The metric directly measures asset-level licensing coverage and counts of unlicensed_install_count (data_filters: site, vendor, compliance_status), which are direct indicators of asset-management risk (R-Asset management).

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A rapid change in the distribution of unlicensed_install_count across site/vendor (the metric's data_groups: vendor, license_status, site; natural_language_question asks where largest concentrations are) would indicate a distribution_shift (e.g., new unlicensed software concentration at a site). No explicit scenarios were provided to map to.

metrics.name: Misconfiguration recurrence rate by device
metrics.description: Measures how often the same misconfiguration (vuln_id) reappears on a device (dev_id) over time, indicating recurring configuration drift or ineffective remediation.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which devices repeatedly exhibit the same misconfigurations and might require configuration hardening or process changes?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: Recurring misconfigs point to maintenance/hardening gaps (VPM-1) and possible change-control failures (CFG-1). The metric's use of first_observed/last_observed and dev_id aligns with these controls' expected data_signals, supporting moderate evidence strength.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Recurrence counts and percent_devices_with_recurrent_misconfigs are direct measurements of configuration drift risk and its operational impact on access/control posture.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: An uptick in percent_devices_with_recurrent_misconfigs above an SLA threshold (KPIs include percent_devices_with_recurrent_misconfigs) signals ineffective remediation and triggers a threshold_breach alert.

metrics.name: Device misconfiguration burden index
metrics.description: A composite index scoring devices by misconfiguration count, severity, and persistence to rank assets by remediation priority and potential operational risk.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which devices contribute most to organizational misconfiguration risk and should be targeted by the next remediation wave?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: The index depends on accurate asset inventory (AST-3 production_asset_inventory_and_lifecycle_management) and maintenance/hardening signals (VPM-1). The metric's source_schemas and dev_id groupings align to AST-3 data_signals and VPM-1 maintenance/patching evidence, giving moderate strength when inventories and patch logs are present.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: The composite burden index is an aggregated proxy for device-level operational risk (combining counts, severity, persistence) rather than a direct incident count; it helps prioritize assets that likely contribute disproportionately to risk exposure.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A concentration of burden (index trend such as 'burden concentration' or sudden rank migration among top devices) would appear as a distribution_shift in index values and indicate a hotspot scenario requiring remediation.

metrics.name: Attack surface impact index
metrics.description: Combines propagation_impact, bastion_impact, category_impact and effective_impact into a single index indicating potential blast radius and attack surface amplification to prioritize containment and segmentation efforts.
kpis: percentage of CVEs in top attack_surface decile
count of high-index CVEs above threshold
metrics.natural_language_question: Which vulnerabilities present the largest attack surface amplification (propagation + bastion effects) and which assets would benefit most from microsegmentation?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: The index is intended to prioritize microsegmentation and containment; NET-2 (network segmentation and zone isolation) and IAC-5 (production OS access restriction) evidence_requirements (asset-to-zone mapping, blocked cross-segment attempts, host access controls) align to the index's purpose and underlying data (propagation/bastion impact).

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: The index is a constructed proxy of blast radius/attack-surface amplification (it infers potential exposure rather than counting actual incidents), so it serves as a proxy indicator for asset and communications-related risk concentrations.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A sudden spike in the attack_surface_index or a rise in the count of CVEs above a high-index threshold (KPIs include 'count of high-index CVEs above threshold') would indicate emergent attack-surface amplification requiring immediate segmentation work.

metrics.name: Propagation impact ratio
metrics.description: Computes the ratio of propagation_impact to category_impact or effective_impact per CVE to identify vulnerabilities whose primary concern is lateral spread rather than local impact, informing containment priorities.
kpis: count of CVEs in top propagation ratio decile
trends: proportion of high-effective_risk CVEs with dominant propagation
metrics.natural_language_question: Which vulnerabilities are most likely to enable lateral movement (high propagation impact relative to local category impact) and where should we strengthen network segmentation?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: NET-2 (network_segmentation_and_zone_isolation) directly maps to NLQ asking where segmentation should be strengthened; IAC-6 (production_network_access_restriction) is relevant because propagation impact depends on network access controls. Strength is moderate because the ratio shows potential lateral risk but requires network flow/firewall evidence for strong control proof.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: The propagation_to_category_ratio is a direct measure of lateral-movement potential per CVE (KPIs explicitly measure percent with ratio > 1 and top decile), directly quantifying information-security operational exposure and asset-level propagation risk.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A CVE whose propagation_to_category_ratio crosses a policy threshold (e.g., ratio > 1) would trigger containment priorities; KPIs include percent with ratio > 1 and top-decile counts, making threshold breaches a clear detection mechanism.

metrics.name: Outdated OS Rate
metrics.description: Measures the proportion and count of assets running operating systems whose release/install date or version indicates end-of-life or unsupported status; helps prioritize upgrade and patching efforts for vulnerable OS fleets.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which sites and device types have the highest proportion of assets running out-of-support or outdated operating systems?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: AST-3 (production_asset_inventory_and_lifecycle_management) requires inventory fields (os_version, lifecycle_status) — the metric's source schema (cvedata/asset_schema) and filters (os_release_install_date) map directly to AST-3 data_signals. VPM-1 (service_infrastructure_maintenance_and_hardening) aligns with remediation/patching evidence (last maintenance/patch dates). Together they provide strong evidence for control compliance.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: The metric directly counts and proportions assets with outdated OS versions (KPIs percent_outdated_os, count_outdated_assets), which is a direct measurement of asset-management and operational security risk indicators.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: An increasing share of assets flagged as out-of-support (distribution shift in os_version/age metrics like median_os_age_days) would indicate growing EOL exposure and a scenario requiring accelerated remediation.

metrics.name: Secure Boot Compliance Rate
metrics.description: Tracks the percentage and count of assets with secure boot enabled versus not enabled, indicating firmware-level protection adoption across the estate; important for assessing foundational platform security posture.
metrics.natural_language_question: What percentage of production servers have secure boot enabled by region and device type?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: NET-5 (configuration_hardening_and_standards) maps to hardening compliance; AST-3 (asset inventory) supplies the required asset metadata (os_name/os_version/roles); END-1 (endpoint_protection_and_malware_defense) is relevant for endpoint-agent presence. Evidence is moderate because secure-boot flags from inventory are strong, but proving enforcement across boot chains may require firmware attestations.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Secure boot compliance is a direct, measurable control attribute on assets (secure_boot_status filter) and therefore directly quantifies platform-integrity risk relevant to cryptographic/firmware compromise and operational security.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A breach of a policy threshold (e.g., secure_boot_enabled_rate falls below policy target in a region/device_type) would indicate increased firmware/boot-level exposure requiring immediate remediation.

metrics.name: Legacy Protocol Exposure (SMB1/SMB2/SMB3)
metrics.description: Counts and profiles assets exposing legacy protocols (e.g., SMBv1) or unexpected SMB configurations to identify protocol-related attack surface and remediation targets.
metrics.natural_language_question: How many assets expose SMBv1 and where are they located across our sites and regions?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: NET-4 (firewall_configuration_and_coverage) aligns to blocking/exposing protocols at the network layer and NET-4's data_signals include firewall exports and flow logs that directly evidence exposure. AST-3 (asset inventory) supplies per-asset fields (os_name/os_version) used by this metric; together they provide strong evidence of protocol exposure and remediation coverage.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: The metric directly counts assets with SMBv1/legacy protocol enabled (KPIs smb1_enabled_count, smb1_percentage) and maps them by site — a direct measurement of communications-security and asset-surface risk.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A sudden increase in assets reporting SMBv1_enabled_count or a shift in geographic distribution (e.g., new site with many SMBv1 assets) would be observed as a distribution shift and indicate emergent protocol-exposure risk.

metrics.name: Reboot Pending Rate
metrics.description: Measures the proportion and count of assets flagged as pending reboot, useful for tracking remediation rollouts that require restarts and for identifying incomplete patch cycles.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which teams and sites have the most assets pending reboot after patching and what is the average time to complete those reboots?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: VPM-1 (service_infrastructure_maintenance_and_hardening) expects patching and maintenance timestamps (data_signals include patch deployment logs) that align with reboot_pending indicators; AST-3 (asset inventory) provides asset lifecycle and reconciliation fields. Evidence is moderate because reboot flags show incomplete remediation but additional change-ticket linkage strengthens proof.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Pending reboots are a contributing factor to ongoing vulnerability exposure and operational risk (they indicate incomplete remediation rather than directly representing a vulnerability count).

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A spike in reboot_pending_rate or avg_pending_duration_days (trends listed: time-to-reboot) would indicate stalled remediation rollouts or deployment failures and should trigger operational follow-up.

metrics.name: Bastion Device Inventory & Risk
metrics.description: Identifies and quantifies bastion/jump-host devices (is_bastion_device) and profiles their locations, uptime, and platform details to prioritize hardening of high-value access points.
metrics.natural_language_question: Where are our bastion hosts located, how many are in each region, and which have the highest uptime or outdated platforms?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: IAC-5 (production_os_access_restriction) explicitly addresses centralized/bastion access enforcement and lists required data_signals (host inventory, authorized accounts) that map to this metric's is_bastion_device and uptime_mins fields. AST-3 supports the asset-inventory evidence required to show bastion hosts are recorded and managed; combined these provide strong evidence.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Counting bastion/jump-host devices and their share of critical assets is a direct measurement of privileged-access exposure and helps quantify access-control related risk indicators.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: If bastion_count or bastion_percentage_of_critical_assets exceeds an expected threshold (e.g., too many unmanaged bastions or bastions with outdated platforms), the metric would trigger a threshold breach indicating elevated privileged-access risk.

metrics.name: Hardware Capacity Profile
metrics.description: Profiles CPU core counts and physical memory across assets to understand capacity distribution, identify underprovisioned systems, and plan workload placement or decommissioning.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which device classes have anomalously low memory-to-core ratios that could indicate performance or misconfiguration risks?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: CAP-1 (system_capacity_review_and_planning) explicitly expects CPU/memory utilization and capacity-review artifacts which align to the metric's KPIs; VPM-1 supports maintenance patching/maintenance evidence that complements capacity planning. Evidence is moderate because capacity distribution indicates compliance but may need review artifacts to be strong.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Anomalous low memory-to-core ratios are contributing factors to performance and availability risk; the metric signals potential operational impact rather than directly measuring a security incident.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A device class showing sustained abnormal memory-to-core ratio or a rising fraction of underprovisioned systems (distribution shift/anomalous cluster) would appear as an anomaly pattern indicating misconfiguration or capacity issues.

metrics.name: Asset Reporting Latency
metrics.description: Measures latency and freshness of asset records by comparing creation/ingestion timestamps (raw_created_at, store_created_at, store_updated_at) to detect stale or slow inventory pipelines.
metrics.natural_language_question: How fresh is our asset inventory per site and which ingestion sources or locations routinely exceed the freshness SLA?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: AST-3 (production_asset_inventory_and_lifecycle_management) requires inventory completeness and reconciliation timestamps (raw_created_at/store_updated_at), which map directly to this metric's data_signals. MON-2 (log_collection_and_retention_coverage) aligns with ingestion and last-received timestamps for logs/pipelines. Strength is moderate because pipeline latency metrics are good evidence but need linkages to corrective actions/audit trails to be strong.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Median_latency_minutes and percent_within_SLA are direct measurements of asset-inventory freshness and therefore directly quantify asset-management and operational inventory risk indicators.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: If percent_within_SLA falls below a policy threshold or median_latency_minutes exceeds SLA, this threshold breach would detect stale/incomplete inventory pipelines as described in the NLQ.

metrics.name: Retired Asset Signal Ratio
metrics.description: Quantifies how many assets marked as retired still report signals (events_timestamp, IP/MAC), indicating stale inventory or orphaned systems that may inflate risk counts.
metrics.natural_language_question: How many assets marked as retired are still reporting network or telemetry signals and where are they concentrated?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: This metric directly evidences AST-3 'production_asset_inventory_and_lifecycle_management' (description & data_signals call for inventory records, owner, lifecycle_status) by surfacing retired assets still reporting; it also maps to AST-2 'data_retention_and_disposal_lifecycle' as retired assets that should be disposed are still emitting telemetry (data_signals: events_timestamp, store_updated_at).

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: The metric directly measures an indicator in the asset management risk domain (retired assets reporting) and is a concrete signal of inventory/lifecycle control gaps that increase operational security risk.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A policy threshold (e.g., retired_reporting_rate > 0 or > X%) would indicate stale/orphaned systems; the metric's retired_reporting_rate and KPIs (average_time_since_last_event_days) would cross that threshold when the scenario occurs.

metrics.name: BIOS/Firmware Diversity & Outdated Rate
metrics.description: Analyzes BIOS vendor/version and release dates to identify common firmware variants and the share of devices running outdated firmware that may lack security fixes.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which device families and manufacturers have the highest share of outdated BIOS/firmware that should be targeted for coordinated updates?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: VPM-1 (service_infrastructure_maintenance_and_hardening) expects measurement of maintenance/patching coverage and overdue assets — percent_outdated_bios is a direct maintenance indicator. NET-5 (configuration_hardening_and_standards) is relevant because firmware baseline/hardening compliance ties to approved standards; alignment is moderate because control evidence often expects patch execution logs in addition to inventory metadata.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Percent_outdated_bios is a proxy for exposure to known firmware vulnerabilities (it doesn't by itself show exploitability/exposure but quantifies susceptibility across device families).

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A sudden shift in the distribution (e.g., one vendor/model account rising to a high percent_outdated_bios) would indicate an emerging mass-exposure or failed coordinated updates; the metric's 'distribution shift' trend supports this detection.

metrics.name: Location & Tag Completeness
metrics.description: Measures completeness of geographic metadata (location_country/region/city) and tagging fields (asset_tags, discoverable_tags, is_manual_canonical_label) to gauge inventory quality and enable reliable grouping and access control.
metrics.natural_language_question: What proportion of assets have complete location and tagging metadata by site and region, and where should tagging efforts be focused?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: AST-3 explicitly requires inventory records with required fields (owner, location, lifecycle_status) — percent_with_location/tags directly evidence that requirement. CPL-1 (system_description_and_documentation_management) is supported because canonical labeling and documentation completeness are part of system description coverage.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Completeness percentages directly measure inventory quality, a primary risk indicator for asset-management risk (missing tags lead to mis-scoped access and delays in remediation).

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: An absence of expected metadata (sharp drop in percent_with_location or percent_with_tags) indicates missing upstream syncing or failed discovery pipelines; the metric's 'completeness improvement trend' KPI supports detecting missing data.

metrics.name: Device vulnerability density
metrics.description: Counts vulnerability instances per device and per software instance and reports density metrics (e.g., vulnerabilities per host). Identifies hotspots where remediation or segmentation effort will have the greatest impact.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which devices or software instances have the highest density of vulnerabilities, and how many of those are high severity?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: None of the provided controls are a one-to-one match explicitly requiring 'vulnerabilities per device' counts; the metric is nevertheless useful for vulnerability programs, but input controls list contains vulnerability-related controls (VPM-2/OPS-1) — however because evidence requirements for those controls require scan coverage and remediation timelines rather than raw density alone, direct mapping is weaker without linked remediation tickets.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Device vulnerability density is a proxy for attack-surface concentration on assets and contributes to asset- and operations-related risk — it does not directly measure exploit likelihood but highlights high-risk hosts.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A rising tail of devices with high vuln_count_per_device (density distribution shift / top-device emergence) would indicate emergent hotspots; trends include 'top-device emergence' and 'density distribution shift'.

metrics.name: Device Participation Rate in Vulnerability Acceptance
metrics.description: Proportion of devices (dev_id or instance_id) that have at least one accepted vulnerability record in a given timeframe, indicating coverage of acceptance across assets.
metrics.natural_language_question: What percentage of active devices have at least one accepted vulnerability record in the past 30 days and is coverage improving?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: AST-3 (production_asset_inventory_and_lifecycle_management) requires mapping discovered assets to inventory and measuring coverage; device participation rate (pct_devices_with_entries, devices_with_no_entries_count) directly evidences inventory coverage and reconciliation as described in AST-3.evidence_requirements.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Device participation rate is a direct measurement of asset inventory coverage and therefore directly quantifies asset-management related risk (unmanaged/uninventoried devices increase exposure).

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: If expected device records stop appearing (pct_devices_with_entries drops or devices_with_no_entries_count rises), this absence_detection signals gaps in collection or discovery (missing coverage) and should trigger investigations.

metrics.name: Patch deployment velocity by process
metrics.description: Counts successful installs per unit time partitioned by process_id to evaluate throughput of different deployment/automation processes. Helps identify fast and slow deployment channels.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which deployment processes (process_id) install the most fixes per day and how has that changed in the last 30 days?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: Velocity per process_id (data_groups include process_id) is relevant to change control (CHG-1) and configuration/change governance (CFG-1) because it reveals process throughput and potential emergency/unapproved channels; it also supports OPS-1's remediation tracking. Because velocity alone doesn't prove approvals or rollback plans, evidence strength is moderate.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Low deployment velocity contributes to prolonged exposure and is therefore a contributing_factor to information security operational risk; KPIs like installs_per_hour are the measured signals.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A sudden drop (or spike) in installs_per_hour or installs_per_day (trends include 'velocity change') would trigger spike_detection indicating pipeline outages, failures, or a process regression needing immediate investigation.

metrics.name: Fix instance duplication index
metrics.description: Measures the rate of duplicate fix records (multiple fix ids for same sw_instance_id and patch_type) to expose data duplication or aggregation issues. Reducing duplication improves accuracy of remediation KPIs.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which software instances show the highest rate of duplicate fix records and from which sources do duplicates originate?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: Duplicate rate ties to asset-inventory reconciliation (AST-3) and ingestion/log collection quality (MON-2) — the source and raw_created_at/store_created_at filters line up with MON-2's ingestion/last-received signals, but duplicates are a data-integrity indicator rather than a direct control action, so evidence strength is moderate.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: High duplicate_rate_per_sw_instance inflates or obscures remediation KPIs and contributes to misestimation of exposure and operational risk (contributing_factor).

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A sudden change in duplicate_rate_per_sw_instance or a new source creating many duplicates (trends include 'distribution shift') would present as an anomaly_pattern in duplication distributions, indicating ingestion/aggregation failures.

metrics.name: Install completion ratio by device
metrics.description: Calculates the ratio of fixes installed to fixes reported for each dev_id to assess device-level remediation completeness. Identifies devices that consistently miss installations.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which devices have the lowest install completion ratios over the last 60 days and what common characteristics do they share?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: The metric's device_install_completion_rate (KPIs) and sw_instance/dev_id grouping directly map to VPM-2 host scan/remediation requirements and OPS-1 vulnerability management evidence; AST-3's asset inventory signals support per-device attribution — together this provides strong evidence for remediation/completeness controls.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Per-device completion rate is a direct measurement of how many assets remain incompletely remediated and therefore directly quantifies exposure for operational and asset-management risks.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: If a device's install_completion_ratio falls below an organizational threshold (KPIs include devices_below_threshold), that threshold_breach indicates devices needing immediate remediation and possible escalation.

metrics.name: Software instance staleness rate by product
metrics.description: Measures the proportion of software instances marked as stale (is_stale) per vendor/product/version. Tracks inventory drift and out-of-support or neglected installations which increase risk and maintenance burden.
trends: distribution shift of staleness by product
metrics.natural_language_question: Which vendors and products have the highest proportion of stale software instances and is that trend improving or worsening?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: AST-3 (production_asset_inventory_and_lifecycle_management) requires a centralized inventory and lifecycle status; this metric uses cvedata/software_instances_schema with product_state/is_stale fields and KPIs like percent_stale_by_vendor — these align directly with AST-3's inventory and lifecycle data_signals (asset records, lifecycle_status, reconciliation timestamps).

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Percent stale instances is a direct indicator of asset-management risk (R-Asset management) because it measures missing lifecycle maintenance; it also directly contributes to Information Security Operations risk (unmaintained software increases exposure).

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A rise in staleness_rate above an organization policy threshold (e.g., percent_stale_by_vendor > policy) would be detected as a threshold breach; a sudden change in the distribution across vendors would also manifest as a distribution shift.

metrics.name: Software patch freshness index
metrics.description: A composite freshness score per instance combining recency of latest_patch_install_time, is_stale flag, and relation to latest_available_patch. Useful as a dashboard KPI to summarize fleet patch health.
metrics.natural_language_question: What is the overall patch freshness score for the fleet and which product groups fall below the freshness threshold?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: AST-3 (inventory/lifecycle) and OPS-1 (vulnerability procedures) are relevant: the freshness index aggregates inventory/patch-install timestamps from software_instances_schema which map to those controls' data_signals; as a composite score, it provides useful evidence but is an aggregated/proxy view rather than raw per-asset records, so evidence strength is moderate.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: The index is a composite proxy that summarizes exposure across many assets (it does not enumerate specific CVEs) so it serves as a proxy indicator for Information Security Operations and Asset Management risk posture.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A sudden shift in the freshness_index distribution (e.g., large vendor groups falling below the freshness threshold) would indicate deteriorating patch health and trigger investigation; crossing the percent_above_threshold boundary would also be a threshold breach.

metrics.name: Software instance state transition rate
metrics.description: Tracks changes in product_state for instances (e.g., installed, removed, deprecated) over time to understand churn, lifecycle progression, and removal/deprecation adoption speed.
trends: median time spent in each state trend
metrics.natural_language_question: At what rate are software instances transitioning to deprecated or removed states for product X?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: AST-3 expects lifecycle status and reconciliation metrics (inventory/lifecycle) and CHG-1/CHG-2 cover change processes; the metric uses product_state and store_updated_at which align to inventory change logs and change request/approval evidence in those controls.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: State transition rate directly quantifies asset lifecycle progress and reconciliation speed (Asset Management risk) because KPIs provide percent transitions and median time-in-state.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A slowdown or abrupt drop in transitions to 'removed' or 'deprecated' (distribution shift in percent_transitions_to_removed_or_deprecated or median_time_in_state) would indicate stalled decommissioning and increased exposure.

metrics.name: Time since last update for stale instances
metrics.description: Calculates how long stale instances (is_stale=true) have gone without updates based on store_updated_at or latest_patch_install_time to prioritize remediation of the oldest neglected instances.
trends: median time-since-update for stale instances over time
percent stale older-than thresholds over time
metrics.natural_language_question: How long have stale software instances gone without updates and which products have the oldest stale populations?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: AST-3 expects inventory and lifecycle timestamps; OPS-1 calls for vulnerability scan/agent coverage and remediation timelines — this metric's source fields (is_stale, store_updated_at, latest_patch_install_time) are the specific data_signals both controls require for evidence.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Median days-since-update and percent exceeding thresholds are direct measures of asset neglect and operational vulnerability exposure (Asset Management and InfoSec Operations risks).

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: If percent_stale_older_than_90_days increases above policy thresholds or the median aging climbs past defined windows, that's a threshold breach used to prioritize remediation and escalate risk.

metrics.name: Duplicate software instance index
metrics.description: Identifies and quantifies duplicated software instance records (same product/version/install_path or multiple process_ids on same dev_id), measuring inventory noise and potential measurement issues.
metrics.natural_language_question: How many duplicate software instance records exist per device and which products contribute most to inventory duplication?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: AST-3 requires inventory accuracy and reconciliation; the duplicate index (based on dev_id/product/install_path/process_id) maps to the inventory reconciliation data_signals. Because this metric highlights data-quality issues rather than directly proving reconciliations, evidence strength is moderate.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: High duplicate rates are a contributing factor to Asset Management risk by inflating or obscuring true asset counts and undermining vulnerability coverage calculations; the metric measures inventory noise rather than direct compromise indicators.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A sudden change in duplicate_instance_count distribution (e.g., a new collector duplication bug creating a spike in duplicates) or persistent high duplicates after cleanup efforts would appear as a distribution shift and indicate measurement problems requiring remediation.

metrics.name: Staleness rate by vendor
metrics.description: Proportion of software instances flagged as is_stale per vendor; helps surface vendors whose deployments are failing to receive updates or attention and therefore may harbor latent vulnerabilities.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which vendors have the highest proportion of stale software instances and how has that proportion changed over the last 90 days?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: This metric maps to AST-3 (production_asset_inventory_and_lifecycle_management) because it uses the software instances inventory (source_schemas: cvedata/software_instances_schema_swkey) and quantifies coverage/currency (is_stale, store_updated_at) required by AST-3; it also supports OPS-1 (vulnerability_management_and_system_monitoring_procedures) as a strong indicator of unmanaged assets that increase scanning and remediation workload (kpis: count_of_stale_instances).

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: The metric directly measures an asset-inventory attribute (is_stale) and its proportion by vendor (kpis and data_groups include vendor/product), directly quantifying asset management gaps and operational security exposure.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: If a vendor's stale_instance_rate (KPI: stale_instance_rate) crosses a policy threshold (e.g., >X%), that indicates a failing maintenance process — the metric would show a threshold breach and trigger prioritization.

metrics.name: Patch compliance rate by partition
metrics.description: Share of instances in a partition whose installed patch equals the latest available patch or whose installed patch release time is up-to-date; used to identify partitions with low patch compliance and prioritize remediation.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which partitions currently have the lowest patch compliance rates and which products are the main drivers of noncompliance?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: Patch_compliance_rate uses authoritative instance metadata (source_schemas: cvedata/software_instances_schema_swkey, fields: latest_available_patch, latest_installed_patch) which maps directly to VPM-1 evidence (patching/maintenance logs) and AST-3 (asset inventory coverage and lifecycle state).

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: The metric directly measures the proportion of systems that are unpatched vs up-to-date (noncompliant_instance_count), which directly quantifies unpatched-system risk indicators.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A partition-level patch_compliance_rate falling below a policy threshold (KPI: patch_compliance_rate; trends: partition-level rank change) would be a threshold breach requiring immediate remediation.

metrics.name: Orphaned software instances ratio
metrics.description: Share of instances that lack a process_id or have not been updated within a defined recency threshold (e.g., 90 days), indicating unmanaged or orphaned software that may be blind spots for security.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: The metric directly measures orphaned inventory items (orphaned_instance_count and orphaned_instance_rate) which directly quantify asset-management risk indicators.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: An orphan detection is triggered when expected process_id or recent update timestamps are absent for an instance (natural_language_question defines 'no process id or no update in 90+ days') — absence_detection fits this observable change.

metrics.name: Time to first install after release by product

count of first-install events over time

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: Measured time between patch release and observed first-install event (source fields: latest_patch_release_time, latest_patch_install_time) provides strong evidence for VPM-1 (maintenance/hardening cadence) and OPS-1 (vulnerability remediation performance).

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Median and minimum time-to-first-install directly measure the earliest adoption latency — a direct indicator of exposure windows and operational responsiveness.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: If median_time_to_first_install_days exceeds an SLA (KPI: median_time_to_first_install_days; trends: time to first install over time), that constitutes a threshold_breach indicating slower-than-acceptable patch adoption.

metrics.name: Update frequency by process
metrics.description: Counts and intervals of store_updated_at events per process_id and per instance to measure automation/operational frequency of software updates and detect flapping or unusually high/low update activity.

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: Frequent update event counts help demonstrate whether changes follow documented change processes (CFG-1) and feed detection/monitoring evidence per MON-1 (intrusion/monitoring coverage). risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Update frequency is a proxy: very high or irregular update rates can indicate flapping or unauthorized automation (risk), but frequency alone doesn't prove maliciousness without additional context (kpis: percent_instances_with_multiple_updates).

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A process producing an abnormal surge in update events (KPI: update_events_per_30_days; trends: distribution shift, count over time) would manifest as a spike_detection event that warrants investigation for flapping or malicious automation.

metrics.name: Open ports per device

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Total_open_ports and percent_devices_above_threshold are direct measures of network attack surface, mapping to operational security risk indicators and enabling prioritization of remediation.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A device crossing a policy threshold (e.g., percent_devices_above_threshold_open_ports) or a sudden increase in open ports for a device would be a threshold_breach that indicates elevated attack-surface risk requiring immediate investigation.

metrics.name: Stale open port rate

metrics.name: Open port turnover rate

metrics.name: Exposed critical ports by IP prefix

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: KPIs (critical_port_instances_by_prefix, percent_prefix_exposed_to_critical_ports) directly quantify how much of the network (asset/prefix) is exposed to critical services — directly measuring communications and asset-related exposure.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: Trends include 'geographic/prefix concentration shift' and 'emergent prefix hotspots' — a change in the distribution (concentration shifting to a new prefix) would indicate a network-segmentation/control degradation or sudden exposure hotspot.

metrics.name: Time since last observed for open ports

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: A high median_time_since_last_seen or low percent_seen_within_7_days contributes to asset-management and operational detection risk (stale inventories reduce detection capability), so the metric quantifies a contributing factor.

metrics.name: Agent duplication rate by device

metrics.name: Agent staleness rate and duration

metrics.name: Agent onboarding latency
metrics.description: Measures time between an agent's first observed event and when it appears in the store (ingestion/registration latency), which helps diagnose pipeline delays and improve time-to-detection.

Because this is a timing metric derived from ingestion events (not an approval artifact), strength is moderate.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Latency is a directly measured time delta (first_seen/raw_created_at to store_created_at) whose increase degrades detection/triage capability, matching the risk indicator for operations.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: If onboarding latency rises toward infinity or new agent records stop appearing in the store (no store_created_at events), that indicates ingestion failure; the metric's percent_onboarded_within_SLA and 'count of late onboardings' trend will reveal an absence of timely records.

metrics.name: Agent source reliability index
metrics.description: Scores data sources (display_source/ref_source_id) on reliability using freshness, staleness rates, and update frequency to prioritize trusted telemetry and triage low-quality feeds.

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: The index aggregates freshness and update-interval signals present in the asset_agents schema that AST-3 expects (inventory/update timestamps) and MON-2's ingestion coverage; because it's a composite score (derived), evidence strength is moderate for controls that require raw inventory or agent-heartbeat logs.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: A low source_reliability_score is a contributing factor to asset-management and ops risks (it does not by itself prove a breach but raises exposure due to unreliable telemetry).

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A sustained drop in source_freshness_score or rank change over time (the metric's specified trends) indicates a distribution shift in telemetry reliability and should trigger investigation.

metrics.name: Agent presence stability index
metrics.description: Quantifies agent presence stability per device by comparing active observation duration to total observation window, highlighting intermittent telemetry or flaky agent installs.

Because this is a synthesized stability score rather than raw presence flags, evidence strength is moderate.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Stability_score quantifies intermittent telemetry (median_on_off_ratio) which directly impacts monitoring and detection capability, matching information-security-ops risk indicators.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A shift in the stability_score distribution (the metric's 'distribution shift (stability score)' trend) — e.g., median_on_off_ratio worsening — indicates systemic agent-flakiness or rollout regressions.

metrics.name: Agent retention cohort by source

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: AST-3 requires measures of inventory/onboarding and reconciliation; the metric's cohort KPIs (retention_7d/30d/90d) directly provide per-source onboarding/retention evidence using first_seen/last_seen and ref_source_id fields.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Retention percentages directly measure how long assets/agents remain in inventory and therefore quantify asset-management risk (coverage gaps) per source.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A sudden change in cohort retention curves or an accelerated retention decay (the 'retention decay rate' trend) indicates a distribution shift that signals onboarding or pipeline regressions for a given source.

metrics.name: Reference source ID conflict index
metrics.description: Measures how often an external reference ID (ref_source_id) maps to multiple internal device IDs or different display_sources, indicating mapping conflicts and data integrity issues.

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: AST-3 expects authoritative inventory and reconciliation evidence; the metric's ref_id_conflict_rate and avg_devices_per_ref_id precisely surface mapping conflicts using ref_source_id/dev_id fields. risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: ref_id_conflict_rate directly measures mapping integrity (percent_ref_ids_multi_source) and thus directly quantifies asset-management risk indicators.

metrics.name: Agent update interval distribution

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Long or variable update intervals contribute to detection gaps and thus are contributing factors to operations risk rather than a single incident indicator.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A sustained change in the update-interval distribution (e.g., median/p95 rising or sudden drops in cadence) signals a distribution shift per the metric's 'distribution shift' and 'sudden drops in update cadence' trends.

metrics.name: Interface staleness rate by site
metrics.description: Measures the percentage of interface records marked as stale for each site to identify locations with outdated inventory; useful for prioritizing discovery and reconciliation efforts.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which sites have the highest proportion of stale interface records and how has that proportion changed over the last quarter?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: stale_interface_percentage produced from asset_interfaces_schema (site/is_stale/updated_at) supports AST-3's inventory completeness and MON-2's collection/retention coverage; as a freshness metric it provides moderate-strength evidence of inventory currency.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Percentage of stale interfaces directly measures inventory currency (asset-management risk) and can indicate communications-security exposure for outdated network interfaces.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: If a site's stale_interface_percentage exceeds a policy threshold (the metric's 'Which sites ... changed over last quarter' focus), that threshold breach signals an urgent discovery/reconciliation need.

metrics.name: Interface update latency distribution
metrics.description: Measures the distribution of latency between observed/raw creation times and canonical store update times to surface delays in ingestion or reporting that affect inventory freshness.
metrics.natural_language_question: How long does it take for interface observations to appear in the canonical store, and which processes or sites exhibit the longest delays?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: The metric directly measures ingestion/store update latency (raw_created_at -> store_updated_at) which MON-2 targets for log collection/retention coverage; AST-3's inventory timestamps are also supported. risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: High update latency contributes to communications and operations risks by delaying canonical inventory availability; the metric's percent_under_24h and p95 indicators quantify that contribution.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: If store_updated_at timestamps stop being recorded (ingestion outage) or percent_under_24h drops precipitously (metric trend 'percent_under_24h'), that absence of timely updates indicates an ingestion failure or pipeline outage.

metrics.name: Unique MAC per subnet index

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Unique MAC counts per subnet are a direct measurement of asset density and inventory anomalies (KPIs: unique_mac_count_per_subnet, avg_macs_per_subnet) which map to asset-management and operational-security risks present in the risk register.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A scenario where inventory is misreported or virtualization suddenly increases would show a distribution_shift (subnet MAC counts deviating from baseline); no scenario IDs were provided to list.

metrics.name: IP conflict/overlap rate by device

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: Operational scenarios (e.g., DHCP misconfiguration or sync failures) would produce a threshold_breach where conflict_rate_percent exceeds an acceptable policy threshold; no scenario IDs were provided to reference.

metrics.name: Manufacturer concentration index (HHI)
metrics.description: Computes a Herfindahl-Hirschman style index over interface records by manufacturer to quantify vendor concentration and diversity across the estate.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A scenario where a single vendor grows disproportionately would appear as a distribution_shift or rising HHI over time (trend: 'market_share_shift'); no scenario IDs provided to attach.

metrics.name: Interface change rate by reporting process
metrics.description: Tracks creation and update frequency of interface records per reporting process_id to surface noisy or overly-aggressive sources and to understand scanner/reporting behavior.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which reporting processes generate the most interface churn and are any sources producing excessive or noisy updates?

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: High/change burstiness is a contributing_factor to operational risk (misconfigured scanners, noisy feeds causing missed alerts) rather than a direct measure of breach likelihood.

metrics.name: Stale interface age distribution

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Age-of-stale-records is a direct measurement of inventory staleness (KPIs and data_filters include is_stale/raw_created_at), which quantifies asset-management risk (out-of-date inventory).

metrics.name: Subnet address utilization ratio

metrics.name: Interface reporting source diversity per device
metrics.description: Counts distinct reporting sources (process_id) per device to measure redundancy and coverage of discovery, highlighting devices with single-source detection risk.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Distinct-source counts per device are a direct measurement of discovery coverage and therefore directly quantify the asset-management risk of single-source detection gaps.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A failure of a reporting pipeline or scanner will show as absence_detection (drop in sources per device or rise in devices_with_single_source_percent); no scenario IDs were provided to list.

metrics.name: MAC manufacturer OUI mismatch rate
metrics.description: Compares the recorded manufacturer field to the vendor inferred from the MAC OUI and reports the mismatch rate to detect metadata quality issues or incorrect inventory labeling.
metrics.natural_language_question: What percentage of interface records have a manufacturer that does not match the OUI-derived vendor from the MAC address and where are these mismatches concentrated?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: AST-3 requires accuracy of inventory attributes and reconciliation ('inventory change logs', 'automated asset discovery/scan results'). The metric uses MAC/OUI alignment from asset_interfaces_schema to demonstrate attribute accuracy, matching AST-3 data_signals.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: The mismatch_rate_percent directly measures metadata quality (KPIs and data_filters include mac, manufacturer), quantifying asset-management risk from incorrect labeling.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A sudden rise in OUI mismatch_rate_over_time would indicate data-source regressions or a new ingestion pipeline producing incorrect manufacturer values (distribution_shift); no scenarios provided to list.

metrics.name: Vulnerability staleness rate by instance
metrics.description: Computes the proportion of vulnerability events marked is_stale per instance (or device) to surface assets with outdated or unresolved event records. High staleness rates indicate potential data quality issues or neglected remediation workflows.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which instances have the highest proportion of stale vulnerability records and how has that changed over the past 90 days?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: The metric uses instance_id/dev_id and tracks event record staleness (data_filters include is_stale, instance_id) which aligns to AST-3's requirement to demonstrate accurate production asset inventory and reconciliation; it also surfaces gaps in vulnerability monitoring aligned to OPS-1. risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: High per-instance stale rates (kpis: percent_instances_with_stale_events, avg_stale_rate_per_instance) indicate poor asset/event hygiene that contributes to asset management and security operations risk (stale data can hide exposures) rather than directly measuring breach likelihood.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A sudden absence or drop in fresh (non-stale) vulnerability updates for an instance (data_filters: raw_created_at/store_updated_at) would show up as increased stale rate — this metric would detect when expected update records stop arriving for an instance.

metrics.name: Event-to-store update latency
metrics.description: Measures the time between raw_created_at (event ingestion) and store_updated_at/store_created_at to quantify processing and ingestion latency. Monitoring latency helps ensure timely visibility for detection and response.
metrics.natural_language_question: What is the median and 95th percentile time from event creation to store update, and which ingest processes contribute most to high latency?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: This metric uses raw_created_at/store_updated_at and process_id (data_filters) to measure ingestion latency which directly aligns to MON-2's log collection/ingestion latency evidence and OPS-1's expectation for timely vulnerability scan/ingest processing; the p95/median KPIs map directly to the evidence requirements.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Ingest latency KPIs (median_ingest_latency, p95_ingest_latency) directly measure operational detection delay risk for security operations (delayed visibility increases detection/response risk).

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: If median or p95 latency crosses SLA (kpis include percent_under_sla_latency) or a particular process_id shows persistent p95 breaches, that threshold breach indicates processing/ingest pipeline failures or backlogs affecting visibility.

metrics.name: Multi patch-type per software instance rate
metrics.description: Percentage of software instances (sw_instance_id) that are associated with multiple patch_type values, signaling fragmentation or inconsistent remediation per instance. High fragmentation can complicate automated rollouts.
metrics.natural_language_question: What proportion of software instances have multiple different patch types recorded, indicating versioning or remediation inconsistency?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: The metric uses sw_instance_id and patch_type (source_schemas: cvedata/fix_instances_schema) to assess per-instance metadata completeness/consistency — AST-3 (production_asset_inventory_and_lifecycle_management) requires accurate software/inventory fields and OPS-1 expects monitoring of remediation coverage; the metric partially evidences both (asset inventory completeness and remediation consistency).

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: High fragmentation is not a direct vulnerability count but contributes to asset management risk (incomplete/ inconsistent inventory and harder remediation), making it a contributing_factor to increased exposure.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: An increase in average_patch_type_count_per_instance or a shift in the patch-type count distribution (data_groups include sw_instance_id, patch_type) would indicate fragmentation/discrepancy growth; no scenario IDs provided to map detection to.

metrics.name: Subscription Option Presence by API Version
metrics.description: Counts and reports which subscription options are present (enabled/declared) in each Qualys API version field. This matters to understand feature footprint and what capabilities are exposed per API form.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which subscription options are present in each API version and how many options does each version expose?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: Subscription option presence and counts per API version (source_schemas: qualys_knowledge_repo/general) provide evidence about system description/documentation completeness (CPL-1) and software/inventory footprint (AST-3); this is moderate strength since controls expect published descriptions and inventory linkage.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: The metric does not measure incidents but contributes to asset/schema management risk (incomplete API feature mapping complicates integrations), so it is a contributing_factor to those risk categories.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: A sudden change in enabled_option_count_per_api_version or percent_options_present_per_api_version across data_groups would be a distribution_shift indicating schema drift or unexpected deprecation; there are no scenario IDs provided to map to.

metrics.name: Deprecated Subscription Option Detection Rate
metrics.description: Identifies options present that are known or inferred to be deprecated (e.g., present in older API fields but absent or replaced in newer ones). This helps plan cleanup and migration of integrations.
metrics.natural_language_question: Which subscription options are deprecated (present only in older API fields) and how many deprecated options are still reported?

control_evidence_hints.reasoning: Detecting deprecated options in qualys_knowledge_repo/general supports system description currency (CPL-1) and change governance (CFG-1) evidence: it surfaces drift and lifecycle exceptions that those controls expect to track.

risk_quantification_hints.reasoning: Deprecated option counts indicate technical debt and integration fragility (asset management risk) but do not directly measure exploitability, so they act as a contributing_factor.

scenario_detection_hints.reasoning: If newer API fields suddenly lack expected options while older fields still report them, an absence_detection (expected options missing in new versions) would reveal incomplete migrations or misconfiguration; no scenarios available to map to.

metrics.name: Subscription Option Naming Variation Index
